loops.description: Reassess returns to Observe when condition shifts, restarting the full cycle
Reassess returns directly to Classify when only the placement is judged stale, not the underlying observation

branches.description: Outcome of Reassess branches conditionally: if condition type shifts, loop to Observe; if initial placement no longer fits but situation is stable, loop to Classify; otherwise exit or continue downstream
branches.condition: condition shift vs. placement mismatch vs. neither

candidate_missing_primitives: Monitor (continuous low-grade observation distinct from a discrete Observe step, implied by 'if the condition shifts')
Diagnose (the blinded text implies a more specific 'what changed' check than generic Reassess provides)
ambiguities: Whether 'Reassess' is itself a primitive action or a compound of Observe+Classify re-run
Whether 'Decide how to respond' implicitly includes an Act step, or Act is left implicit/out of scope of the blinded text
Whether condition-type placement is a one-time Classify or could involve iterative Compare/Evaluate against multiple candidate types before settling
No explicit Verify, Communicate, or Coordinate primitives appear in the blinded text, though the real-world artifact this resembles often involves multi-party coordination
notes: Blinded text describes a four-stage core loop (Observe→Classify→Interpret→Decide)
